description: One bounded piece of work, identified by the action and what it acts on. Whether anyone assigned or accepted it, and whether it is worth doing, belongs in further clauses. Purposeful work is 'riola' (labor); 'riporu' is the unit that can be planned or tracked.
articulatory_notes: A tongue tap carries 'ri' into high 'i'. Stressed 'po' closes at the lips and rounds; final 'ru' returns to tongue contact before deep 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The word shares 'ri' with 'riola' (labor), an intentional family resemblance. That echo gives the two work words a small aid to memory.
usage_notes: The noun is countable. A performer can be designated with 'rilowa' (assign), while 'kelomi' (accept) records acceptance; neither relation is built into the task itself.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Shared maintenance can be divided into tasks while useful activity remains wider than jobs and commodities.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Separating a task from its assignment and acceptance keeps an expected action from being mistaken for consent.

semantic_domains.activity: one bounded unit of intended or discussed work
